claimText: Bank interest rates have an inverse relationship with existing fixed-coupon bond prices; an increase in interest rates leads to a fall in bond prices.
evidenceExcerpt: When bank interest rates rise, existing fixed-coupon bond prices fall
examUse: Statement-based question on macroeconomic linkages and RBI rate hike impacts.
distractorHooks: Direct relationship between interest rates and bond prices
Bond prices remain unaffected by bank interest rates

claimText: An increase in bank interest rates causes the effective yield of existing fixed-coupon bonds to rise due to the drop in their market prices.
evidenceExcerpt: When bank interest rates rise, existing fixed-coupon bond prices fall, causing their effective yield to rise.
examUse: Statement-based question on bond yield dynamics.
distractorHooks: Bond yields fall when bank interest rates rise
Bond yields are independent of bond prices

claimText: Higher prevailing bank interest rates make existing bonds with fixed lower coupons less desirable, which drives down their market price.
evidenceExcerpt: Higher interest rates make existing bonds with fixed lower coupons less desirable, dropping their price.
examUse: Conceptual clarity question on why bond prices fluctuate in the secondary market.
distractorHooks: Higher interest rates make existing fixed-coupon bonds more desirable
Lower interest rates make existing fixed-coupon bonds less desirable
